title: Hub-App — Internal Operations
essence: Hub-app is the operations tool that ~11 internal users live in for hours a day. Calm, dense, get-out-of-the-way. Operations-room dark by default with a high-contrast light mode for field laptops. Brand orange is reserved for action — never decoration. Information density is high. If it feels like a marketing site, it is wrong. If it feels like a senior advisor's notebook — orderly, dense, marked-up where it matters — it is right.
audience: ~11 internal users across Operations, Sales, Compliance, and Drivers. Roles where the app is open all day and one extra click adds up.

Brand.name: Primary
Brand.role: The one action this view exists for. Buttons, key links, selected-row indicator.

Brand.name: Accent (Cyan)
Brand.role: Non-action emphasis — status, metric highlights. Never an action colour.

Surfaces (Dark default).name: Surface
Surfaces (Dark default).role: Cards, modals, drawers, sticky table headers.

typeScale.label: body
typeScale.sample: Stock arrives at site Tuesday 06:00. Driver is John. Margaret has acknowledged.

fonts.use: All UI type. Workhorse sans for screens.
fonts.why: Inter is the field workhorse — Proxima Nova is a print/marketing font and isn't worth the licensing complexity for an 11-user internal tool. Renders crisply at 12–14px which is most of what hub-app shows.

fonts.use: IDs, batch numbers, codes, JSON, anything copyable.
fonts.why: Tabular numerals stop column drift in dense tables. Distinguishable 0/O and 1/l/I — drillers paste batch IDs all day.

do: Use --primary for the one action a view exists for.
Render every state — default, hover, focus, loading, empty, error, permission-denied.
Use status roles (success/warning/danger/info) for state — never orange.
Default to dark theme. Light is the field/outdoor mode.
Use tabular numerals in tables and metric strips.
Render PDS/SDS in-app to capture the audit trail.
dont: Don't show a spinner over an empty table — use skeletons.
Don't render 'No data' with no follow-up action.
